payload.claim: What is Markdown? Why was it invented? What is it's lineage?
Markdown is a lightweight markup language for creating formatted text using a plain-text editor. It was created by John Gruber in 2004 with Aaron Swartz as a sounding board.

The goal of Markdown was to enable people 'to write using an easy-to-read and easy-to-write plain text format, optionally convert it to structurally valid XHTML (or HTML)'. Readability was the primary design goal, unlike heavier markup languages like RTF, HTML, or wikitext.

Markdown's lineage was inspired by pre-existing conventions for marking up plain text in email and usenet posts, as well as earlier lightweight markup languages like setext (c. 1992), Textile (c. 2002), and reStructuredText (c. 2002).
